## [00:00 - 03:02]

In this video, we're going to go over some of the online space lingo. Now you obviously might be familiar with some of this already, but I just want to go over a couple of things that have come up, have come through, and we just want to make sure that you fully understand what it is that people are saying and how to go about it essentially, and really just so you don't understand. So the first one here is just offer. So really it's just basically the program and what it is that you're selling. So for example, for us, Closetintel leaders, we have three different offers, which obviously you learned about earlier on in this program or in these modules. So that's basically the offer. Then we have the done, sorry, DWY and then DFY. So done with you or done for you. These are obviously the acronyms. So done with you. So really this is more of like a module slash course plus some sort of like group coaching or one-on-one offer. So an example of a done with you offer is our RCA offer as well as even STA and 7FSA. These are all done with you offers. And then we have done for you, which obviously that person does or that company does everything. So we have done for you recruiting, where we essentially have all of the reps that we pre-screen and then we just simply make the group or make the connection and we go and do that whole process for the company. So we have done for you recruiting, for example. From there, no, there's a PIFS, split pay, three pay. So PIFS is just an acronym for paid in full. This just means that the full investment was paid upfront. Split pay is usually when the investment is split into two. So 50% upfront on that first phone call and then the rest later, typically after usually a month, maybe 45 days, but it could also be a little bit longer, but that's just the average there. Then there's a three pay where the investment is then split into three payments. So you get one third upfront, another third probably the next month and then last third probably the month after that. You can go into four pays and five pays and different payment plans, but those are kind of like the main three here. From there, there's also sales mentor and traffic and funnels. So a lot of the people that you may be talking to have gone through sales training from sales mentor or traffic and funnels. So Taylor Walsh and Chris Evans, they also have, with the E-Line Wild, they also have similar program to RCA, so just basically sales training. And a lot of people, as I said, that we interview have been through one of their trainings. Now they have a couple of different levels, if you will. There's the accelerator, the certification, I believe the private coaching. So I believe the accelerator is $97. Then the certification is $500 and the private coaching is $8,800. So whenever someone says they've been through sales mentor training, I always ask them which level they've gone through and how much they paid for it so that obviously we have a little bit of a better idea as to how much they've invested into it or how much they've committed to it based on that. And then really from there, OTE, you might see this acronym thrown around.

## [03:02 - 06:05]

It basically just means on-track earnings basically for that month, what someone can make in any given month. And then this here is going to be cash collected versus revenue though. This number is going to be very, very important when you go through and you have the interview asking questions and we'll touch on this a little bit down below, but revenue and cash collected are going to be two different things. So cash collected is obviously the amount of cash that you collect where the revenue is generated essentially. So this is going to be a little bit of an example here for you. So let's say the offer, whatever you're selling, let's say RCA, let's say RCA is called 5K. So let's say there's a closer, the closer gets 20 closes that month, 10 of those closes pay in full, five of them end up slitting the payment into two, so it split pays and then you get five, three pays. So the total revenue generated is going to be $100,000, 20 times five obviously gives you $100,000 or 20 times 5,000 gives you $100,000. So the business is getting, so that closer generated $100,000 in revenue for that business. Now that $100,000 is going to come isn't going to come in in that first month. It'll come in eventually, but it won't be all right away. So that's what cash collected is. So cash collected is physically the amount of cash that you or that closer collected on that given month. So if you have for example, 10 paid in full closes times 5,000, that's $50,000 in cash collected. Then they have five split pays times 2,500, half of the 5K upfront, that's another 12,500 obviously altogether. Then you have five, then that closer got five, three pays, which then means 1.6K, so obviously one third of the 5K upfront, that obviously gives us 8K. So if we add all these up here, it gives us a total of $70,000, $70,500 in cash collected for that month. Well the revenue generated was $100,000. Now the closer then gets paid commission on this number here, the total number of cash collected, not revenue generated. So this is going to be a super, super clear distinction that you have to make sure that you get because someone might say, hey, you know I've generated a million dollars with revenue for this business in the past year, but the cash collected might only be a quarter of a million because the rest of that money isn't going to come until later on because it's on a contract. So that's a super, super, super clear distinction that you'll have to make later on when you're taking the interview, but this is just super, super important for you to know. And then from there, this is close rate, so obviously there's close rate, live close rate, offer to close rate, set to close rate, so obviously close rate, percentage of people closed. Live close rate is percentage of people that the closer talked to that closed, offer to close rate is a percentage of people that got offers that then ended up closing. Set to close rate is basically for setters, which is the percentage of people that closed

## [06:05 - 06:19]

from the sets that the setter got. That's pretty much it here. These are some of the main online space lingo terms that you'll probably see thrown around. If there's any that you see that you don't know or you're not familiar with, by all means just let us know and we'll help you out.
